one_time_description: Comprehensive land claim negotiation loan debt of $4,391,871 forgiven by the Federal Government of Canada
economic_notes: Band operates through 10 government business partnerships including marina, forestry, fisheries, environmental assessments, shopping centres, residential holdings, wood trucking, timber management, and grizzly bear tourism. COVID-19 noted as subsequent event expected to materially impact 2021 surplus and GBPs.
notes: Fiscal year 2020 (ended March 31, 2020). Prior year (2019) had a qualified audit opinion due to a qualified opinion on one GBP's financial statements for 2018 and 2019. The 2020 audit opinion is clean. Band has 10 GBPs accounted for by equity method. A $4,391,871 land claim negotiation loan was forgiven by the federal government in 2020 — treated as one-time item. No schedule of remuneration included in the statements provided. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($3,562,314) + Ottawa Trust Funds ($6,099) + CMHC ($107,828) + FNHA ($76,758) + Province of BC ($413,416) + School District 72 ($52,258) = $4,218,673; however School District 72 may be considered OSR context — total_government_transfers set to $4,160,374 excluding School District 72 from government transfers or $4,218,673 including it. Used $4,160,374 excluding School District 72. Employment Programs segment also reported: expenses $262,229, revenue $199,661.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
